post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 238



It’s a normal day at UnpaidInternCentralTM – BossTM was HuntingDailiesTM, HannahWTM was putting periods at the end of her ThonksTM, MathletesvTM was being a GoodStudentTM, mathjayTM was ProcrastinatingTM – everything was perfectly normal.

Suddenly, BossTM brings in a new BotTM. Everyone gasps.

“What’s this?” HannahWTM asks.

“Well, you see, we have a problem,” BossTM begins dramatically, “It has been brought to my attention that our UnpaidInternsTM are getting too much sleep!”

Everyone shrieks in horror. One UnpaidInternTM even passes out.

“Ahem, as I was saying, people are getting too much sleep an-”

More blood-curdling screams and people fainting.

“OH, I ADMIT IT,” screams HannahWTM as she falls to the ground, “I’ve been sleeping more than 2 hours a day!”

“NO! Get away from me!” exclaims MathletesvTM, who sits right next to her, as he immediately gets up from his chair and scrambles to the other side of the room. He looks a little green.

“Yep, and this new BotTM will solve the problem!” says BossTM, triumphantly, “Behold, TheOneAndOnlyTM ReaperBotTM! With this handy little BotTM, everyone will be staying up till 4 AM for NightReapsTM!”

“So, uh, how does this reap thing work?” asks MathletesvTM, as he randomly reaps 24 seconds off the clock, “Oh wait, oops, I didn’t mean to do that.”

“Figure it out yourself! By the way, whoever wins the most games at the end of the year gets a raise of 0 dollars!”

And so the ReaperWarsTM began.

As BossTM looked on, he was proud to see the bags under the eyes of the UnpaidInternsTM. He was proud to know that none of them was sleeping more than half an hour per day. And most importantly, he was proud to find that the TopSecretTM SecurityCamerasTM that he installed in the bedrooms of all the UnpaidInternsTM had not been discovered.

- Important Rules -
This tab summarizes the most important rules.

For experienced players: this is the only tab you need to read.
For new players: if you have time, familiarizing yourself with the rest of the rules can be helpful.

Integrity
  • Do not edit or delete your posts, even for typos. Just post again.
  • Do not OOT (Out of Thread) and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request.
  • Do not gamethrow or intentionally sabotage your wincondition.
  • Do not attach files or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
  • Do not use OGI (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games.

Activity
  • Each phase lasts 10 days.
  • Do not post during night.
  • This game uses Dawn start; Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules.
  • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable.
  • Activity rules are 48 hours to in-game prod and 72 hours to PM prod.

Voting
  • You must format your votes correctly. Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
  • Deadline hang and majority hang are always on.

Etiquette and Warnings
  • Use common sense.
  • Do not use formatting that makes text hard to read.
  • You may attack a player’s opinion, but do not make personal attacks on players themselves.
  • First-timers can receive up to 3 warnings before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. All other players can receive up to 2.
  • All other rules are standard. If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.

Resources and Support
  • For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (s0c00b0#5989).
  • For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below.
  • If you have understood the all rules, please make a post that includes the phrase "potato injection."
  • For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go here.
  • The New Player Crisis Manual (Mk 1) is automatically PMed to all new players. A public version of the NPC Manual can be found here.
  • If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM.

  • General
    Credits to Binomial-theorem, chaotic_iak, willwang123, and fdas, among others, as well as the Mafia Universe forum rules, for the rule template.

    The following are standard mafia game rules.

    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
    • Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent].
    • Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON.
    • If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered.
    • Do not impersonate the mods.
    • Do not lie to the mods.
    • Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement.
    • You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.

  • Votes and Hangs
    This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.

    • Votes must be in this format: Vote: [player username] to be counted.
    • Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames.
    • For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical.
    • For example, both Vote: mathjay and Vote: mAtHjAy are valid votes, but vote: mathjay, Vote: jay, Vote: mathjay., Vote:mathjay, Vote: mathjay, and Vote: mathjay are invalid.
    • Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid.
    • Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
    • No Hang is not allowed in this setup.
    • Unvote by posting Unvote. You do not have to unvote before voting again.
    • Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
    • Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
    • This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
    • If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If no votes have been cast by the deadline, a random person will be hanged.
    • If a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately.
    • The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
    • Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.
  • Activity
    This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.

    • All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
    • Each phase will last 10 days.
    • The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
    • If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
    • Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
    • Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline.
    • Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in bold in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded.
    • V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options.
    • Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting Request Replacement in-thread or in their role PM.
    • Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in.
    • Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
    • Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA.
    • Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement.
    • If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
    • If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
    • Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart.
    • If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
    • If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.
  • Integrity and Etiquette
    This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.

    • Do not gamethrow.
    • Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators.
    • Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
    • Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative.

    The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, please click here.

    • Do not make personal attacks.
    • Do not spam.
    • Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning.
    • Follow AoPS Community Guidelines.
    • Be mature and use common sense.
    • For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.

    The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, please click here.

  • Moderation Policies
    This tab codifies several moderation procedures.

    • Role Randomization

    When randomizing roles for the game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. Both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are thus possible.

    Current RNG Process (Purgatory)
    Once the playerlist has been finalized, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, then Mafia Goon. This list will be randomized by the List Randomizer and merged with the playerlist, which is ordered by descending rank, then by alphabetical order. The game's ordered playerlist is in the Alive/Dead tabs.


    • Tiebreaking

    If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made in Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made in Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This avoids tiebreaking via RNG while preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting.

    • Votes cast during deadline

    If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the officially logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[player ID] and referencing its upper-right corner.

    Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes logged as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not count.

    • Mafia Night Kill Disagreement

    The moderators encourage the Mafia members to discuss the night kill using the private chat and agree upon a kill. If the players cannot arrive at a conclusion by the night deadline and continue to be in disagreement, however, the mafia factional kill will not go through. The moderators will not arbitrate this disagreement.

    • Game-advancing Posts

    A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. Most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks.

    • Postbans

    Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, moderators cannot proxy posts from postbanned players. If a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation if the postban is less than a week in length.

    Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help.

    You may contact the moderators through discord at ash in june#5081 or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS.

    • Outside Consulting

    If needed, moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.

    • Out Of Thread

    Due to clarifications by fdas, strictly mentioning procedural aspects of this game, such as game number, rules, player names, will not be OOT. However anything beyond that, such as flavor, gamestate, gameplays, will be OOT.

  • Moderator Information
    This tab provides technical information about this game.

    • Socoobo is the moderator of Mafia Game 238.
    • This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate.
    • Red text is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
    • Blue text is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.
    • Green text is used for town flavor-related text.
    • Custom shades of these colors may be used for aesthetic purposes.
    • Current custom shades are: Town flavor green (#00f752), Activity Check blue (#005dff), mathjay's blue (#4a86e8), HannahW's blue (#0b5394), Mathletesv's blue (#4d94ff), Scipio1's blue (#3d70ff), Socoobo's orange (#ff8000).
    • The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
    • If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History.
    • Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules.
    • Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History.
    • The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game.

    MG238 OP is based on Overcomplicated RulesetTM (OCRSTM) version 8.
    Current version: 8.7
    Version History
Setup
This game is based on the Purgatory open setup.

Roles
  • 6 Vanilla Townies
  • 3 Mafia Goons

Description
  • Instead of day and nights, phases alternate between PromotionTM and FiringTM. The game starts with a FiringTM Phase. All phases have 10-day deadlines.
  • During PromotionTM Phase, the town votes to PromoteTM. During FiringTM Phase, the town votes to FireTM.
  • Town wins if the majority of scum is FiredTM, scum wins if the majority of scum is PromotedTM.
  • Alignment is revealed upon being PromotedTM or FiredTM.
  • All scum, whether alive or not, can talk to each other at all times in the mafia QT.
  • Only townies who are FiredTM are added to the dead QT. Townies who are PromotedTM have a separate ManagerTM QT.
  • When 50% of the living players are scum, a JudgmentTM Phase occurs. All living players lose the ability to vote. The ManagersTM privately vote in the ManagerTM QT to FireTM a living player. If the player is town, the mafia win. If not, the game moves on to a FiringTM Phase.
  • Compulsive plurality eliminations! Ties are broken by checking whoever got their last vote first.

Role PMs

You have no special abilities or skills; your power lies in your observational skills, your voice, and your vote. You win when a majority of the mafia have been FiredTM.

Good luck!
Mafia Goon wrote:

Your partners are [name] (Mafia Goon) and [name] (Mafia Goon). You may talk to them privately in [QT link] at any time, even if you have been PromotedTM or FiredTM. You win when two members of the mafia have been PromotedTM or when the mafia make up a majority of the currently living players.

Good luck!

Automatic ISO Code
Written by casi and improved by Mathletesv. Reposted with permission.

ISO Code
Number.prototype.between = function(a, b, inclusive) {
  var min = Math.min(a, b),
    max = Math.max(a, b);
min -=1, max += 1;
 
  return inclusive ? this >= min && this <= max : this > min && this < max;
}
function number_matches(quote_box,start,end) {
return (parseInt($(quote_box).parents(".cmty-post-tr").children(".cmty-post-middle").children(".cmty-post-top-inline.cmty-post-top-data").children(".cmty-post-top").children(".cmty-post-number").text().slice(1)).between(start,end) )
    }
function user_matches (quote_box,username) {
    return ($(quote_box).parent().parent().children(".cmty-post-left").children(".cmty-post-username").text() == username)
    }

user = prompt("What is the username of the person you are iso'ing?");
min = parseInt(prompt("First number of range"), 10);
max = parseInt(prompt("Second number of range"), 10);
$(".cmty-post-quote").each(function () {
    if (user_matches(this, user) && number_matches(this, min, max)) {
        $(this).click()
    }
    })


Source


Description: given a username and a range of posts to look in, this code will automatically quote all posts from the specified player into the reply field.

Note: this code does not work on Android, iOS, or any other device without a javascript web console.

Instructions
Step 1: Load the posts you want to ISO.
The code cannot retrieve a player’s posts if you do not have them currently loaded in your browser, so you must scroll down (or up) through the thread to load the posts you want in your ISO. AoPS periodically refuses to load posts when scrolling up; if this happens, “bounce” the thread by scrolling down for a bit before scrolling back up.
Step 2: Open your browser’s console.
For Chrome, the shortcut is Control + Shift + J (Windows, Linux, Chrome OS) or Command + Option + J (macOS). For Safari, first enable Developer Menu by going to Safari Menu > Preferences > Advanced tab and checking “Show Develop menu in menu bar,” then use the shortcut Command + Option + C. For Firefox, the shortcut is Control + Shift + K (Windows, Linux) or Command + Option + K (macOS). For Microsoft Edge, the shortcut is Control + Shift + J.
Step 3: Copy/paste the code into the console and hit Enter.
Once you do so, the code will prompt you in a popup, and you will not be able to access the tab you are running the code on. Do not switch to another tab in the same window, or the code will not work.
Step 4: Enter the username of the player you are ISOing.
This is case-sensitive; capitalize and spell the username correctly.
Step 5: Enter the first post of your ISO range.
If you are doing a full ISO, you can use 1 or 0. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number from which you want the code to search for a player’s posts.
Step 6: Enter the last post of your ISO range.
If you are doing a full ISO, enter any number larger than the thread postcount; 10000 works. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number to which you want the code to search for a player’s posts. Please note that posts that are within the range you have specified, but that have not been loaded in your browser, will not be included by the code in the ISO.

Once you have done so, the reply field will open, with all the relevant posts quoted in it.


Important notes:
  • The username is case sensitive.
  • The min/max limits of the range do not have to be exact.
  • The first range input must be smaller than the second range input.
  • This code does not work in [pywindow].
  • You cannot access the contents of the tab you are running the code in while it is running, so remember to memorize the target username before running the code.
  • You can rerun the code once it has finished running.
  • You can run the code even if you have a reply partly written.
  • If the code refuses to work, check that all of the above has been done correctly. If it still does not work, reload the tab and try again.

For further troubleshooting, please PM the moderators of the game you are in.


Game is currently in progress. If you are not playing in this game, please do not post. You can go to this thread to sign up.

Game is over.
Congratulations to the scumteam of NDMath, Scipio1, and falliinqsnow- on winning Mafia Game 238 (UIC Invitational II).

It’s a normal day at UnpaidInternCentral[sup]TM[/sup] – Boss[sup]TM[/sup] was HuntingDailies[sup]TM[/sup], HannahW[sup]TM[/sup] was putting periods at the end of her Thonks[sup]TM[/sup], Mathletesv[sup]TM[/sup] was being a GoodStudent[sup]TM[/sup], mathjay[sup]TM[/sup] was Procrastinating[sup]TM[/sup] – everything was perfectly normal.

“What’s this?” HannahW[sup]TM[/sup] asks.
 
“Well, you see, we have a problem,” Boss[sup]TM[/sup] begins dramatically, “It has been brought to my attention that our UnpaidInterns[sup]TM[/sup] are getting too much sleep!”
 
Everyone shrieks in horror. One UnpaidIntern[sup]TM[/sup] even passes out.
 
“Ahem, as I was saying, people are getting too much sleep an-”
 
More blood-curdling screams and people fainting.
 
“OH, I ADMIT IT,” screams HannahW[sup]TM[/sup] as she falls to the ground, “I’ve been sleeping more than 2 hours a day!”
 
“NO! Get away from me!” exclaims Mathletesv[sup]TM[/sup], who sits right next to her, as he immediately gets up from his chair and scrambles to the other side of the room. He looks a little green.
 
“Yep, and this new Bot[sup]TM[/sup] will solve the problem!” says Boss[sup]TM[/sup], triumphantly, “Behold, TheOneAndOnly[sup]TM[/sup] ReaperBot[sup]TM[/sup]! With this handy little Bot[sup]TM[/sup], everyone will be staying up till 4 AM for NightReaps[sup]TM[/sup]!”

“Figure it out yourself! By the way, whoever wins the most games at the end of the year gets a raise of 0 dollars!”
 
And so the ReaperWars[sup]TM[/sup] began.
 
As Boss[sup]TM[/sup] looked on, he was proud to see the bags under the eyes of the UnpaidInterns[sup]TM[/sup]. He was proud to know that none of them was sleeping more than half an hour per day. And most importantly, he was proud to find that the TopSecret[sup]TM[/sup] SecurityCameras[sup]TM[/sup] that he installed in the bedrooms of all the UnpaidInterns[sup]TM[/sup] had not been discovered.

[hide=- Important Rules -]
[b]This tab summarizes the most important rules.[/b]

[b]For experienced players:[/b] this is the only tab you need to read. 
[b]For new players:[/b] if you have time, familiarizing yourself with the rest of the rules can be helpful. 

[b]Integrity[/b]
[list][*][b]Do not edit or delete[/b] your posts, even for typos. Just post again. 
[*][b]Do not OOT (Out of Thread)[/b] and talk about this game outside your role PM, QTs, approved Discord channels, or this thread, even if you are dead. Access to the spectator chats will be granted upon request. 
[*][b]Do not gamethrow[/b] or intentionally sabotage your wincondition. 
[*][b]Do not attach files[/b] or link to external documents concerning this game. Discussions must stay in this thread.
[*][b]Do not use OGI[/b] (Out of Game Information). Publicly discussed reads should be made exclusively from information in this thread or past completed games. [/list]

[b]Activity[/b]
[list][*][b]Each phase lasts 10 days.[/b]
[*][b]Do not post during night.[/b]
[*]This game uses [b]Dawn start;[/b] Day 1 will not start until everyone shows that they have read the rules. 
[*]All [b]times[/b] will be in [b]Eastern Standard Time[/b] (EST, UTC-5) or [b]Eastern Daylight Time[/b] (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable. 
[*]Activity rules are [b]48 hours to in-game prod[/b] and [b]72 hours to PM prod.[/b][/list]

[b]Voting[/b]
[list][*]You [b]must format your votes correctly.[/b] Capitalization doesn't matter, and capital i/lowercase L are not differentiated.
[*]Deadline hang and majority hang are always on.[/list]

[b]Etiquette and Warnings[/b]

[list][*][b]Use common sense.[/b] 
[*]Do not use formatting that makes text [b]hard to read.[/b]
[*]You may attack a player’s opinion, but [b]do not make personal attacks[/b] on players themselves. 
[*]First-timers can receive up to [b]3 warnings[/b] before their next offense results in a force replacement or modkill. [b]All other players can receive up to 2.[/b]
[*][b]All other rules are standard.[/b] If you break a rule that isn't covered here, you may still be warned.[/list] 

[b]Resources and Support[/b]

[list][*]For any issues, you may contacts us through your Role PM or through Discord (s0c00b0#5989). 
[*]For a list of basic resources, please refer to the Resources tab below.
[*]If you have understood the all rules, please make a post that includes the phrase "potato injection."
[*]For an more extensive list of resources compiled by the UIMT, please go [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c1980342h2517106_important_resources]here[/url].
[*]The New Player Crisis Manual (Mk 1) is automatically PMed to all new players. A public version of the NPC Manual can be found [url=https://artofproblemsolving.com/community/c1980342h2517631_new_player_crisis_manual_8]here[/url].
[*]If this game is causing you significant stress or frustration, feel free to use your role PM to talk or vent. We may not be allowed to give you technical advice, but we can listen or help you find a better way to process events. You may also contact us via Discord if you prefer; you can ask us about this in your role PM.[/list][/hide]

[list][*][hide=General]
[i]Credits to [url=http://bit.ly/fbith]Binomial-theorem[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmchao]chaotic_iak[/url], [url=http://bit.ly/rmww123]willwang123[/url], and [url=http://bit.ly/ffdas]fdas[/url], among others, as well as the [url=http://bit.ly/MUnFR]Mafia Universe forum rules[/url], for the rule template.[/i]

[b]The following are standard mafia game rules.[/b]


[list]
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from the mods, including role PMs.
[*]Do not talk about this game's events outside of this thread unless you are specifically given permission to do so by the moderators. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]Do not talk about other ongoing games in this thread. Referring to anything that has happened in an ongoing game, even if you do not say which game it is, is OOT.
[*]Do not use any formatting that obscures or conceals text. This includes [verbatim][color=#FFFFFF], [color=white], and [color=transparent][/verbatim]. 
[*]Do not edit or delete posts, even for typos: what goes in the thread stays in the thread. Instead, use EBWOP or EBWON. 
[*]If you are dead, do not post, and do not talk about this game anywhere else. You may still spectate, and access to the spoiled and unspoiled spectator chats will be offered. 
[*]Do not impersonate the mods. 
[*]Do not lie to the mods. 
[*]Do not abuse loopholes in the rules. Intent will be considered during rule enforcement. 
[*]You may use your role PM to ask questions, such as rule clarifications.[/list][/hide]

[*][hide=Votes and Hangs]
[b]This tab covers voting, unvoting, No Hangs, and hangs.[/b]


[list][*]Votes must be in this format: [b]Vote: [player username][/b] to be counted.
[*]Usernames in votes must be spelled, but not necessarily capitalized, correctly. Do not use nicknames. 
[*]For the purposes of voting, I (capital i) and l (lowercase L) are considered identical. 
[*]For example, both [b]Vote: mathjay[/b] and [b]Vote: mAtHjAy[/b] are valid votes, but [b]vote: mathjay[/b], [b]Vote: jay[/b], [b]Vote: mathjay.[/b], [b]Vote:mathjay[/b], Vote: [b]mathjay[/b], and [b][i]Vote: mathjay[/i][/b] are invalid. 
[*]Votes followed by extraneous punctuation that is bolded, even with separate bold tags, are invalid. 
[*]Votes in hide, tip, or quote tags do not count.
[*][b]No Hang is not allowed in this setup.[/b]
[*]Unvote by posting [b]Unvote[/b]. You do not have to unvote before voting again. 
[*]Invalid votes will not count under any circumstances. Please consider notifying the mods in your role PM if you want to purposely fakevote.
[*]Valid votes will always be counted unless they are clearly unintentional. There is no guarantee that an accidental vote will not be counted.
[*]This game uses deadline hang: the player with the most votes at the EOD (End of Day) deadline will be hanged.
[*]If two or more players have the same number of votes when the deadline is reached, the player who reached that number of votes first will be hanged. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If no votes have been cast by the deadline, a random person will be hanged.
[*]If a player is voted by the majority of living players, then that player will be hanged immediately.
[*]The moment the deadline occurs, no further votes will be counted. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab. 
[*]If the deadline has passed or a hammer has occurred and the EOD has not been declared, the game will proceed into twilight. Players may still post, but no more votes or unvotes will be counted.
[*]Once the moderators declare the EOD, the person hanged will be officially eliminated and their role revealed. Night will then immediately begin.[/list][/hide]
[*][hide=Activity]
[b]This tab covers deadlines, activity rules, V/LAs, and replacements.[/b]


[list][*]All times will be in Eastern Standard Time (EST, UTC-5) or Eastern Daylight Time (EDT, UTC-4) as applicable, unless otherwise noted.
[*]Each phase will last 10 days.
[*]The moderators may extend deadlines at their discretion.
[*]If the moderators do not receive an NA submission from a player by the night deadline, the player will not perform a NA that night.
[*]Night results will be sent after the night deadline.
[*]Night will end when the moderators make a post declaring the end of night. You may not post in the thread until then, even if the night deadline has passed. Instead, PM the mods for updates in the case of a missed deadline. 
[*]Players who are temporarily unable to fulfill activity requirements can declare a V/LA (Vacation/Long Absence) by posting the V/LA dates in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. Players on a V/LA will not be prodded. 
[*]V/LAs should be shorter than one week. For longer absences, contact the moderators for more options. 
[*]Players who no longer wish to or are unable to play in this game can request replacement by posting [b]Request Replacement[/b] in-thread or in their role PM. 
[*]Players who have replaced out generally will not be allowed to replace back in. 
[*]Players will receive an in-game prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 48 hours and are not on V/LA. Details on "game-advancing post" are in the Moderation Policies tab.
[*]Players will receive a PM prod if they have not made a game-advancing post for 72 hours and are not on V/LA. 
[*]Players will be replaced if they have not made a game-advancing post for 108 hours and are not on V/LA, unless they post before the moderators find a replacement. 
[*]If a player receives 4 PM Prods, they will be replaced.
[*]If a player reaches replacement status more than once, they will be replaced.
[*]Activity will be reset overnight. Players will not receive prods between twilight and daystart. 
[*]If you have access to a private topic, you may request that a partner be poked. The moderators will send a notification via PM to the specified player, but this will not count as an official prod.
[*]If a player is postbanned by the AoPS Sheriff, they will be replaced. Details are in the Moderation Policies tab.[list][/hide]
[*][hide=Integrity and Etiquette]
[b]This tab covers integrity recommendations, player etiquette, warning limits, and examples of inconsiderate gameplay.[/b]


[list][*]Do not gamethrow. 
[*]Do not discuss player alignments based on V/LAs, replacements, warnings, force replacements, modkills, and other interactions with moderators. 
[*]Any significant, game-related, and intentionally exclusive inter-player communication outside of moderator-sanctioned channels is prohibited.
[*]Do not publicly reference OGI (Out of Game Information) in reads or create situations outside the thread that players could reasonably think as alignment indicative. [/list]

The above rules are attempts to preserve the spirit of the game and should not affect most players. For specific details and examples, [url=https://aops.com/community/p21335214]please click here.[/url]


[list][*]Do not make personal attacks.
[*]Do not spam. 
[*]Behavior that disrupts the game for players and makes it unreasonably hard for them to read and play may result in a warning. 
[*]Follow AoPS Community Guidelines. 
[*]Be mature and use common sense. 
[*]For minor rule infractions, first-timers can receive up to three warnings, while all other players can receive up to two. It is possible to receive multiple warnings for a single infraction.[/list]

The above rules broadly cover most etiquette issues, but for specific details and examples of inconsiderate gameplay, [url=https://aops.com/community/p21335246]please click here.[/url][/hide]

[*][hide=Moderation Policies]
[b]This tab codifies several moderation procedures.[/b]


[list][*]Role Randomization[/list]

When randomizing roles for the game, the moderators will not “reroll” or otherwise engage in intentional RNG manipulation. Both newbie/newbie and SE/SE scumteams are thus possible. 

[hide=Current RNG Process (Purgatory)]
Once the playerlist has been finalized, the roles will be listed alphabetically in the following order: VTs, then Mafia Goon. This list will be randomized by the [url=https://www.random.org/lists/]List Randomizer[/url] and merged with the playerlist, which is ordered by descending rank, then by alphabetical order. The game's ordered playerlist is in the Alive/Dead tabs.[/hide]


[list][*]Tiebreaking[/list]

If there is a tie at deadline, the player on whom the last vote was made first will be hanged. For example, if the most recent vote on mathjay was made in Post #2106 and the most recent vote on HannahW was made in Post #2132, mathjay will be hanged in the case of a tie. This avoids tiebreaking via RNG while preventing hang manipulation by a single player unvoting and revoting. 


[list][*]Votes cast during deadline[/list]

If votes or unvotes are cast near the EOD deadline, the moderators will reference the officially logged post times. These can be found by locating the relevant post at aops.com/community/u[player ID] and referencing its upper-right corner. 

Votes posted on or after the deadline will not be counted. For example, if the deadline is at 2:00 PM, votes logged as being posted at 2:00 and 2:01 will not count. 


[list][*]Mafia Night Kill Disagreement[/list]

The moderators encourage the Mafia members to discuss the night kill using the private chat and agree upon a kill. If the players cannot arrive at a conclusion by the night deadline and continue to be in disagreement, however, the mafia factional kill will not go through. The moderators will not arbitrate this disagreement. 


[list][*]Game-advancing Posts[/list]

A "game-advancing post" is anything other than an activity post, ie. a post solely made for the purposes of dodging a prod. Most posts other than "activity post" or "hi i got prodded bye" will be counted in activity checks. 


[list][*]Postbans[/list]

Due to clarifications by the AoPS Sheriff, moderators cannot proxy posts from postbanned players. If a player is postbanned, they will be replaced. This may be subject to negotiation if the postban is less than a week in length. 

Please note that we are only prohibited from proxying posts from a postbanned player. If you have trouble accessing AoPS for any other reason, we will do our best to help. 

You may contact the moderators through discord at [b]ash in june#5081[/b] or by editing posts in your role PM if you are unable to post on AoPS. 


[list][*]Outside Consulting[/list]

If needed, moderators may contact a forum or other respected mod for help resolving an incident that could seriously impact game integrity. If you are ever involved in such an incident and disagree with the moderators' course of action, you may request the moderators to consult a second opinion, if they have not done so already.


[list][*]Out Of Thread[/list]

Due to [url=http://bit.ly/fmooc]clarifications[/url] by fdas, strictly mentioning procedural aspects of this game, such as game number, rules, player names, will not be OOT. However anything beyond that, such as flavor, gamestate, gameplays, will be OOT.
[/hide]

[*][hide=Moderator Information]
[b]This tab provides technical information about this game.[/b]


[list][*]Socoobo is the moderator of Mafia Game 238. 
[*]This game uses the Unpaid Intern Central consolidated private chat system. Participants of this game will be granted access to the server as appropriate. 
[*][color=#FF0000]Red text[/color] is used for votecounts, warnings, and other announcements by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related text.
[*][color=#0000FF]Blue text[/color] is used for activity checks, confirm counts, and other non-essential posts by the moderators.

[*]The Not Voting section should be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount.
[*]If an error in the votecount is not pointed out within 2 days or by the next EOD, whichever is sooner, the incorrect votecount will become official.
[*]The moderators may change the rules at any time. Major rule changes will be announced at the bottom of the OP. All changes will also be noted in the modlog and OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]Players will not be retroactively penalized for violating new rules. 
[*]Major changes to the OP may be made in between games. The most important changes will be noted in OCRSTM Version History. 
[*]The moderators pledge to treat players fairly and keep moderator interventions to a minimum; however, we reserve the right to make the final judgement in the moderation of this game. [/list]

[hide=Setup]
This game is based on the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Purgatory]Purgatory[/url] open setup.

[b]Roles[/b]
[list][*]6 [color=#0f0]Vanilla Townies[/color][*]3 [color=#f00]Mafia Goons[/color][/list]

[b]Description[/b]
[list][*]Instead of day and nights, phases alternate between Promotion[sup]TM[/sup] and Firing[sup]TM[/sup]. The game starts with a Firing[sup]TM[/sup] Phase. All phases have 10-day deadlines.
[*]During Promotion[sup]TM[/sup] Phase, the town votes to Promote[sup]TM[/sup]. During Firing[sup]TM[/sup] Phase, the town votes to Fire[sup]TM[/sup].
[*]Town wins if the majority of scum is Fired[sup]TM[/sup], scum wins if the majority of scum is Promoted[sup]TM[/sup].
[*]Alignment is revealed upon being Promoted[sup]TM[/sup] or Fired[sup]TM[/sup].
[*]All scum, whether alive or not, can talk to each other at all times in the mafia QT.
[*]Only townies who are Fired[sup]TM[/sup] are added to the dead QT. Townies who are Promoted[sup]TM[/sup] have a separate Manager[sup]TM[/sup] QT.
[*]When 50% of the living players are scum, a Judgment[sup]TM[/sup] Phase occurs. All living players lose the ability to vote. The Managers[sup]TM[/sup] privately vote in the Manager[sup]TM[/sup] QT to Fire[sup]TM[/sup] a living player. If the player is town, the mafia win. If not, the game moves on to a Firing[sup]TM[/sup] Phase.
[*]Compulsive plurality eliminations! Ties are broken by checking whoever got their last vote first.
[/list]

[hide=ISO Code][code]
Number.prototype.between = function(a, b, inclusive) {
  var min = Math.min(a, b),
    max = Math.max(a, b);
min -=1, max += 1;
 
  return inclusive ? this >= min && this <= max : this > min && this < max;
}
function number_matches(quote_box,start,end) {
return (parseInt($(quote_box).parents(".cmty-post-tr").children(".cmty-post-middle").children(".cmty-post-top-inline.cmty-post-top-data").children(".cmty-post-top").children(".cmty-post-number").text().slice(1)).between(start,end) )
    }
function user_matches (quote_box,username) {
    return ($(quote_box).parent().parent().children(".cmty-post-left").children(".cmty-post-username").text() == username)
    }

user = prompt("What is the username of the person you are iso'ing?");
min = parseInt(prompt("First number of range"), 10);
max = parseInt(prompt("Second number of range"), 10);
$(".cmty-post-quote").each(function () {
    if (user_matches(this, user) && number_matches(this, min, max)) {
        $(this).click()
    }
    })
[/code]

[b]Description:[/b] given a username and a range of posts to look in, this code will automatically quote all posts from the specified player into the reply field. 

[i]Note: this code does not work on Android, iOS, or any other device without a javascript web console.[/i]

[hide=Instructions]
[b]Step 1: Load the posts you want to ISO.[/b]
The code cannot retrieve a player’s posts if you do not have them currently loaded in your browser, so you must scroll down (or up) through the thread to load the posts you want in your ISO. AoPS periodically refuses to load posts when scrolling up; if this happens, “bounce” the thread by scrolling down for a bit before scrolling back up. 
[b]Step 2: Open your browser’s console. [/b]
For Chrome, the shortcut is [b]Control + Shift + J[/b] (Windows, Linux, Chrome OS) or [b]Command + Option + J[/b] (macOS). For Safari, first enable Developer Menu by going to Safari Menu > Preferences > Advanced tab and checking “Show Develop menu in menu bar,” then use the shortcut [b]Command + Option + C[/b]. For Firefox, the shortcut is [b]Control + Shift + K[/b] (Windows, Linux) or [b]Command + Option + K[/b] (macOS). For Microsoft Edge, the shortcut is [b]Control + Shift + J[/b]. 
[b]Step 3: Copy/paste the code into the console and hit Enter.[/b]
Once you do so, the code will prompt you in a popup, and you will not be able to access the tab you are running the code on. Do not switch to another tab in the same window, or the code will not work. 
[b]Step 4: Enter the username of the player you are ISOing. [/b]
This is case-sensitive; capitalize and spell the username correctly. 
[b]Step 5: Enter the first post of your ISO range.[/b]
If you are doing a full ISO, you can use 1 or 0. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number from which you want the code to search for a player’s posts. 
[b]Step 6: Enter the last post of your ISO range.[/b]
If you are doing a full ISO, enter any number larger than the thread postcount; 10000 works. If you are doing a partial ISO, enter the post number to which you want the code to search for a player’s posts. Please note that posts that are within the range you have specified, but that have not been loaded in your browser, will not be included by the code in the ISO. 

Once you have done so, the reply field will open, with all the relevant posts quoted in it. [/hide]

Important notes: 
[list][*] The username is case sensitive. 
[*] The min/max limits of the range do not have to be exact. 
[*] The first range input must be smaller than the second range input. 
[*] This code does not work in [verbatim][pywindow][/verbatim].
[*] You cannot access the contents of the tab you are running the code in while it is running, so remember to memorize the target username before running the code. 
[*] You can rerun the code once it has finished running. 
[*] You can run the code even if you have a reply partly written. 
[*] If the code refuses to work, check that all of the above has been done correctly. If it still does not work, reload the tab and try again. [/list]

[b]For further troubleshooting, please PM the moderators of the game you are in.[/hide]

As the ReaperBotTM got settled in at UnpaidInternCentral, BossTM decided it was time to begin the InvestigationTM. Checking the statistics, he was shocked to see that many of his UnpaidInternsTM had no NightreapsTM! Immediately, in the GreatPurgeOf2021TM, BossTM FiredTM these SleepEnthusiastsTM who clearly had a TerribleWorkEthicTM.

Then, one day, the ReaperBotTM breaks down. Everyone starts running around the building in panic.

“OH NO,” screams Scipio1TM as he looks on in horror, “SOMEONE SABOTAGED THE REAPERBOTTM!”

“Don’t worry, I’ll take care of it,” BossTM replies as he walks into the room, “NOW GET BACK TO WORK OR YOU’RE FIREDTM!”

A few days later…

The ReaperBotTM has been missing for days and everyone has been quite worried. Suddenly, BossTM walks into the building with two people walking behind him.

“Attention everyone!” BossTM exclaims, “I have an important announcement to make! We have two new UnpaidInternsTM!”

Everyone looks away, uninterested.

“No, wait! These are no ordinary UnpaidInternsTM! They can fix the problem with the ReaperBotTM!”

“Oooooooh, really?” asks Scipio1, who was looking to extend his WinStreakTM to 92332143895 games.

“Of course! I have found that among the 9 of you, there are 3 BotSaboteursTM!”

Everyone gasps. mathjayTM faints.

“To my left is the PromotionAngelTM, who will promote people to ManagerTM; to my right is the FiringDevilTM, who will FireTM people! The 9 of you and these two wonderful UnpaidInternsTM will work together to FireTM the BotSaboteursTM!”

“What are you gonna be doing, BossTM?” asks ad333TM.

“Ah, just relaxing in HawaiiTM and taking NoReponsibilityTM for anything that happens in the company during my ConvenientAbsenceTM,” BossTM replies, “Good luck to y’all, and if anything happens, just call me at NaN-NaN-NaNTM where a MonotoneRoboticVoiceTM will misinterpret anything you say until you give up!”

BossTM proceeds to stroll out of the office at a leisurely pace.

“Well, I-” both the PromotionAngelTM and the FiringDevilTM said in unison. They frowned at each other.

“No, I’m going first,” they both said together.

SoonTM, the argument became an all out brawl between the two new UnpaidInternsTM.

“OW, YOU BROKE MY WING!” screamed the PromotionAngelTM.

“YOU BROKE MY PITCHFORK,” wailed the FiringDevilTM, “I PAID A FEW MILLION BUCKS FOR THAT!”

“A few million bucks for a pitchfork? Where’d you buy it, UnpaidInternCentralTM? Only they would make such overpriced pitchforks,” replied the PromotionAngelTM.

Everyone was FiredTM and the game has ended. Congratulations to nobody for winning.

Ok, im genuinely salty you guys quickhammered. It goes against the moral fiber of forum mafia and shows a clear cut disrespect for your fellow players. You aren’t better than anyone else. Quickhammering as a strategy during an ongoing game is fine, even if annoying. It’s advancing your win condition in a fair way. But it’s simply disrespectful as a game-ending move. Taking advantage of the majority mechanics to end the game is just unfair. Like i’m mooing sorry but it’s truly not okay to use one or two wrong townies to end a game. That’s such an inconsiderate way to end the game. It turns into a button-clicking simulator where whoever votes or unvotes the fastest wins. It goes against what forum mafia is and should be, and instead turns it into something that is fun for nobody, just to ‘win’. BobDBuilder321, snoozingnewt, you are what is killing forum mafia. Your selfish actions are morally reprehensible and single-handedly turned what was a good, relatively clean game into something that will be remembered for being nothing. You permanantelnalty marred a good game because you believed yourself to be better than everyone else. Dunning-kroger, anyone? Lol.

You will regret this day and these actions forever. It had been 5 days and the UnpaidInternsTM have yet to come to a consensus. They were still having a friendly discussion at the round table. Everyone was nice and polite. The FiringDevilTM was getting quite BoredTM.

“I need some coffee,” he said as he got up from his chair and went downstairs.

Five minutes later…

The FiringDevilTM was whistling a playful tune as he came back upstairs. The coffee this morning was especially good. He opened the door and saw the entire room on FireTM. One person was backed up against the wall while the others pelted UnpaidComputersTM at him.

“Sorry, wrong room,” the FiringDevilTM said as all the heads in the room turned towards him, “I was looking for room 238, not room 204.”

Huh, I could’ve sworn that room 238 was right there, he thought.

He walked past and saw that the next room was 239. Then he walked in the other direction and saw 237.

Well, that’s a weird place to put 204, right in the middle of 237 and 239… wait a second.

He came back to the original room.

“G- guys? W- what happened? What the heck is-”

“Down with the mathjayTM!” BobDBuilder321TM shouted.

“He’s the imposter!” screamed snoozingnewtTM.

“Alright, alright, calm down,” the FiringDevilTM said, “mathjayTM, you’re FiredTM.”

“NOOOOOOOOO!” mathjayTM screamed, “I’m calling BossTM right now, he’ll un-FireTM me!”

"Hello," said the MonotoneRoboticVoiceTM, "You have reached BossTM's phone. However, he is currently taking a vacation in Hawaii and taking NoResponsibilityTM for what happens at UnpaidInternCentralTM. Please leave a message after the beep."

“NOOOOOOOO-” mathjayTM screamed again as the FiringDevilTM grabbed him by the collar and dragged him out of the room.

mathjay was FiredTM. He was an UnpaidInternTM (Vanilla Townie).

“You come in here and take care of this mess,” the FiringDevilTM yelled as he called the PromotionAngelTM.

The PromotionAngelTM came in and instantly restored the room to its original state.

“Now, let’s do something more pleasant, children,” the angel said, “Somebody’s getting PromotedTM to a ManagerTM!”

Everyone cheered.

FiringTM Phase 1 has ended and PromotionTM Phase 1 has started on July 26, 2021 at 9:00:00 AM EST. PromotionTM Phase 1 will end in 10 days on August 5, 2021 at 9:00:00 EST. Deadline hang and majority hang are both on.

post_rendered:
The FiringDevilTM was slamming his head on his desk as he watched mathjayTM go through the FiveStagesOfBeingFiredTM.

First stage: denial. The devil watched as mathjayTM was dragged out of the building by the security guards for the sixteenth time.

"I WORK HERE, GUYS!" screamed mathjayTM, "STOP KICKING ME OUT!"

He received a sharp punch to the nose and fell unconscious.

Five minutes later...

Second stage: anger. The FiringDevilTM was expecting something dramatic from this, but to his surprise, nothing happened.

He should've known that it wouldn't last. mathjayTM was back, with a flamethrower in his hands.

"SCREW ALL OF YOU!1!1!!11!1!!"

The UnpaidFireDepartmentTM was called in for help, and mathjayTM was arrested for attempted arson.

Three minutes later...

Third stage: bargaining. The FiringDevilTM looked out the window as mathjayTM broke free from the clutches of the UnpaidPoliceTM and took out his phone.

"BOSSTM! I PROMISE I'LL STOP PROCRASTINATINGTM IF YOU'LL UN-FIRETM ME! I SWEAR ON MY LIFE! PLEASE! NO! DON'T GIVE ME THE SILENT TREATMENT! GO AWAY YOU STUPID MONOTONEROBOTICVOICETM!!!1!1!!!1! FINE, NOT ONLY WILL I WORK FOR FREE, I'LL GIVE YOU MY LIFE SAVINGS! BOSSTM-"

The sound of a taser stopped the screaming.

Two hours later...

Fourth stage: depression. The FiringDevilTM received a phone call asking him to come to the local UnpaidJailTM. He walked through the facility until he found mathjayTM sobbing in his cell.

"I'm s- sorry *sniff* My life is t- terrible *sniff* EVERYTHING SUCKS," mathjayTM wailed as he broke into tears once again.

The FiringDevilTM sighed.

Two minutes later...

Fifth stage: acceptance. mathjayTM suddenly stopped crying and beamed.

"You know what? I accept that I'm FiredTM! I never wanted to work for no pay anyway!" mathjayTM shouted exuberantly.

Suddenly, the FiringDevilTM received a phone call.

"We had a wonderful time," said the PromotionAngelTM, "We promoted snoozingnewtTM. Now it's your turn."

snoozingnewt was PromotedTM. He was an UnpaidInternTM (Vanilla Townie).

The FiringDevilTM thought long and hard about resigning before having to put up with another one of these FiveStagesTM but finally sighed and got back to work.

The PromotionAngelTM was introducing snoozingnewtTM to his new ManagerQuartersTM. It had a HugePoolTM, a 65 inch UnpaidTelevisionTM, and a LuxuriousKitchenTM.

“Wait, there’s even a GiganticBedTM?” asked snoozingnewtTM, confused, “What’s that for?”

“SleepingTM, silly!” answered the PromotionAngelTM, “However, if you sleep in it for more than half an hour a night, the UnpaidInternCentralTM SecuritySystemTM will record it and you will be FiredTM by BossTM!”

“Alas, I don’t feel comfortable here,” declared snoozingnewtTM.

“Why?” asked the PromotionAngelTM, confused.

“This is a QuickTopicTM level suite,” snoozingnewtTM said, “I would like to upgrade to a DiscordTM level room.”

The PromotionAngelTM sighed and left the room to check for the availability of the DiscordTM level suites.

Five minutes later…

“GOOD NEWS!” boomed the PromotionAngelTM when she comes back to the room, “We have one, follow me.”

The DiscordTM level room turned out to be even more luxurious, but before they could tour it, suddenly, an alarm blared and the lights flashed red.

“ATTENTION ALL MANAGERS!” the MontoneRoboticVoiceTM said, “THERE IS AN EMERGENCY IN ROOM 238!”

“Oh shoot,” said snoozingnewtTM as he rushed over to the room, seeing a midwaybearTM in tears and the FiringDevilTM looking quite bored.

“NOOOOOOOOOOOOOOO!” screamed midwaybearTM as the FiringDevilTM sighed and dragged him out of the room.

“What? Me? But-”

“Shut up, snoozingnewtTM!” the FiringDevilTM said as he taped shut the mouth of snoozingnewtTM, “Alright, now we can explain how it works!”

“snoozingnewtTM will act as a dictator and decide which one of you to FireTM! If he FiresTM a BotSaboteurTM, then it will be a FiringTM Phase again. Otherwise, YOU’RE ALL FIRED!”
